hero.title: 5 Days, Shimla & Chandigarh.
hero.titleAccent: The Queen of Hill Stations and the Garden City.
hero.intro: The cool colonial charm of Shimla, once the summer capital of British India, paired with the modern green calm of Chandigarh. Pine forests, the Mall Road, and a toy train ride into the Himalayan foothills.
hero.heroImageAlt: Shimla hill station colonial Mall Road Himalayas

overview.heading: 5 Days Shimla & Chandigarh: The Full Picture
body: Shimla was the summer capital of British India, and its colonial heritage lingers in the Mall Road, the Ridge, the mock-Tudor buildings and the cool pine-scented air. Chandigarh, by contrast, is India's first planned city, green, orderly and modern, designed by Le Corbusier. Together they make an easy, scenic Himalayan-foothills break.

This 5 day Shimla and Chandigarh package from Malaysia is private throughout, with your own vehicle and driver guide. You stroll the famous Mall Road, ride the scenic Kalka-Shimla toy train, and explore Chandigarh's gardens and modernist landmarks, a relaxed introduction to the hills of Himachal.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Shimla's Mall Road & the Ridge
items.body: The pedestrian Mall Road and the open Ridge are the heart of Shimla, lined with colonial buildings, cafes and views over the pine-clad hills.

items.title: The Kalka-Shimla Toy Train
items.body: The UNESCO-listed narrow-gauge railway winds through 100 tunnels and countless bridges into the hills, a charming and scenic ride.

items.title: Chandigarh's Gardens
items.body: The Rock Garden, the Sukhna Lake and the green, orderly layout of Le Corbusier's planned city offer a modern, relaxing contrast to the hills.

days.title: Arrive Chandigarh
days.description: Met at Chandigarh airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at Sukhna Lake.

days.title: Chandigarh, on to Shimla
days.description: Chandigarh sights, then drive up to Shimla (toy train option).
suggestedActivities: Drive or toy train to Shimla

days.title: Shimla Sightseeing
days.description: A full day exploring Shimla and surroundings.

days.title: Shimla Leisure
days.description: More of Shimla, with time to relax or explore nearby viewpoints.

days.title: Return & Depart
days.description: Descend to Chandigarh for your onward flight.

whyBook.heading: Himachal's Foothills, Made Easy
whyBook.body: The mountain roads and the toy train timings reward local knowledge. We handle the drives, the train and the stays so the trip is smooth and scenic. Dhesu has run North India tours for over 35 years.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing

sections.heading: Shimla & Chandigarh Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 5 day package is an easy, scenic introduction to the Himalayan foothills, pairing the colonial hill station of Shimla with the modern garden city of Chandigarh.

A private vehicle and driver guide, plus the option of the famous Kalka-Shimla toy train, make it a relaxed trip through pine forests and cool mountain air.

For more of the hills, see our Manali and combined Shimla-Manali packages.

sections.heading: Shimla: The Queen of Hill Stations
sections.body:
Shimla served as the summer capital of British India, and its colonial legacy is everywhere: the mock-Tudor architecture, the Christ Church on the Ridge, the Viceregal Lodge and the bustling Mall Road.

At over 2,000 metres, the cool climate and pine forests made it the perfect summer escape, and it remains one of India's most beloved hill stations.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Chandigarh International Airport (IXC). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Shimla?
items.answer: March to June is pleasant and popular as a summer escape. December to February brings snow, beautiful but cold, with occasional road disruptions. The monsoon (July to September) is lush but can bring landslides on hill roads. Spring and autumn are ideal.

items.question: Is the toy train included?
items.answer: The Kalka-Shimla toy train can be included as part of the journey, subject to availability. Seats are in demand, so we book ahead where possible. We will confirm the segment in your itinerary.

items.question: How cold does Shimla get?
items.answer: Shimla is cool year-round given its elevation, with warm jackets needed in winter when it can snow. Summers are mild and pleasant, a refreshing escape from the plains. Pack layers whatever the season.
